segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Listen to a talk in a biology class.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: The phenomenon of animal migration is a fascinating subject in biology.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Migration involves the regular, often seasonal, movement of animals from one region to another, typically in search of food, breeding grounds or better climates.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: This behavior is observed in a wide variety of species, including birds, mammals, fish and insects.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: One of the most well-known examples is the migration of monarch butterflies.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: These butterflies travel thousands of miles from North America to central Mexico to escape the cold winter.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: This journey involves multiple generations, as no single butterfly makes the round trip.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Another remarkable example is the migration of humpback whales.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: These massive creatures travel from their feeding grounds in polar waters to warmer tropical regions where they breed and give birth.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Birds like the Arctic tern undertake one of the longest migrations, traveling from the Arctic to the Antarctic and back each year.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Despite their differences in size, species and reasons for migrating, they all migrate vast distances with precision.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: They navigate like seasoned human pilots flying transcontinental routes, only without aircraft, instruments or GPS.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Of course, migration is not without its challenges.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Animals must navigate vast distances, often facing predators, adverse weather, and human-made obstacles like buildings and roads.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Protecting habitats and reducing human interference can help maintain the delicate balance that allows migration to occur.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Understanding animal migration provides insight into the complex behaviors and survival strategies of various species.
